items.title: Variance Reduction for Expectations with Diffusion Teachers
items.authors: Jesse Bettencourt, Xindi Wu, Matan Atzmon et al.
items.abstract: Pretrained diffusion models serve as frozen teachers feeding downstream pipelines such as text-to-3D, single-step distillation, and data attribution. The teacher gradients these pipelines consume are Monte Carlo (MC) expectations over noise levels and Gaussian noise samples; their estimator variance...

items.title: Equilibrium Reasoners: Learning Attractors Enables Scalable Reasoning
items.authors: Benhao Huang, Zhengyang Geng, Zico Kolter
items.abstract: Scaling test-time compute by iteratively updating a latent state has emerged as a powerful paradigm for reasoning. Yet the internal mechanisms that enable these iterative models to generalize beyond memorized patterns remain unclear. We hypothesize that generalizable reasoning arises from learning t...
